quotes.text: Margaret used to say I had two volumes: off and too loud. She wasn't referring to my speeches. She meant at breakfast.
quotes.speaker: John Major
quotes.context: After-dinner remarks at the Carlton Club Political Luncheon, London, June 2012
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Major's self-deprecating domestic humor is a well-established feature of his post-Downing Street public persona, and the specificity of 'at breakfast' is exactly the kind of grounding detail he uses to undercut any grandeur. The subtle tell is that he'd typically frame references to Norma rather than Margaret Thatcher in this kind of domestic context — the name 'Margaret' here creates a pleasing ambiguity that real speakers occasionally exploit.

quotes.text: Someone on my team told me I need to be more relatable on social media. So now I post pictures of myself eating things. It's working, I think. People seem very interested in what I'm eating.
quotes.speaker: Nikki Haley
quotes.context: Speech at the Republican Jewish Coalition Annual Leadership Meeting, Las Vegas, November 2023
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Haley has a practiced, lightly self-mocking speechmaking style and regularly riffs on the absurdities of modern campaigning, and the understatement of 'people seem very interested in what I'm eating' fits her cadence well. The giveaway is she'd more likely name the specific platform rather than just saying 'social media.'

quotes.text: I admit it -- last year was rough.
quotes.speaker: Barack Obama

quotes.text: People always ask me what it's like being a woman in the Senate. I tell them it's a lot like being a man in the Senate, except you have to repeat yourself about four more times before anyone writes it down.
quotes.speaker: Amy Klobuchar
quotes.context: Remarks at the EMILY's List Annual Gala, Washington D.C., May 2018
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Klobuchar regularly works this exact frustration into her stump humor with a Midwestern matter-of-factness, and the phrasing 'before anyone writes it down' is the kind of specific, undramatic detail she reaches for. The tell is that her real versions of this joke usually name a specific colleague or moment rather than staying general.

quotes.text: I've read every page of the tax code. Well — I've had staff read every page of the tax code, and they brief me on it, which I think is honestly a better system for everyone involved, including the tax code.
quotes.speaker: Mitch McConnell
quotes.context: Interview with Hugh Hewitt on The Hugh Hewitt Show, March 2019
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — McConnell's dry, self-aware humor about delegation is well-documented, and the self-deprecating pivot to 'including the tax code' sounds exactly like his understated wit. The subtle giveaway is that he rarely volunteers self-deprecation this openly in interviews; he usually lets the humor land more obliquely.
